metadata.title: AI-Driven Smart Citizen Complaint Platform  using Arabic NLP and Clustering
metadata.description:
The Smart Citizen Complaint Platform is an AI-driven complaint management system designed 
to enhance the efficiency of collecting, analyzing, prioritizing, and routing citizen complaints. 
The platform integrates Arabic Natural Language Processing (NLP), complaint clustering, and 
multi-factor reliability scoring to reduce manual workload and support faster and more accurate 
decision-making in public service environments. 
The system accepts complaint text along with metadata such as category, area, date, and location. 
After secure OTP-based user verification, the platform applies transformer-based Arabic NLP 
models to automatically classify complaints and estimate their urgency 
The system architecture is role-based, involving citizens, employees, and administrators. Citizens 
can submit and track complaints, employees handle and update complaint status, and 
administrators monitor reports, system statistics, and operational performance through dynamic 
dashboards and interactive heat maps. 
An experimental evaluation was conducted using Arabic complaint datasets to assess the 
performance of transformer-based models. The results showed that AraBERT achieved the best 
performance with an accuracy of 0.9167 and an F1-score of 0.9167, outperforming MARBERT 
(accuracy 0.910, F1-score 0.909). The experiments also revealed that minimal preprocessing 
combined with AraBERTPreprocessor provides the most effective setup, while aggressive 
preprocessing techniques negatively impact performance due to loss of contextual information.

 

This work was conducted at Arab International University (AIU), Syria. The official website of the university is: https://www.aiu.edu.sy

metadata.title: Improved copy-move forgery detection through multilevel  clustering
metadata.description: Copy move forgery detection (CMFD) based on keypoints remains a widely used technique; however, it often struggles to effectively identify small and smoothly tampered regions within images. This paper introduces a CMFD method that enhances detection accuracy by integrating a double-matching process with advanced region localization techniques. Delaunay triangles formed by accelerated KAZE (AKAZE) and scale-invariant feature transform (SIFT) features are matched in the double-matching process to identify suspicious regions. To ensure sufficient keypoint pairs, the set of matching triangles is iteratively expanded to include neighboring triangles, covering the entire tampered area. Subsequently, a second matching with a looser threshold is performed on the vertices. In the region localization process, the multilevel density-based spatial clustering of applications with noise (DBSCAN) effectively handles scenarios involving multiple copied regions with varying sizes. Using the standard MICC-F600 and COVERAGE datasets, experiments demonstrate that the proposed CMFD method is robust and achieves better performance than state-of-the-art baselines.

metadata.title: A Spatio-Temporal Prediction and Decision Support  Framework for Civic Issue Hotspots using LSTM  Networks and Spatial Clustering
metadata.description:
Civic platforms collect large amounts of data from citizens regarding issues in their communities. However, most existing systems focus only on visualizing reported problems rather than analyzing their recurrence or suggesting long- term solutions. This paper proposes a system that predicts the likelihood of civic issue recurrence and recommends effective long-term interventions. The system leverages Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to capture temporal patterns in issue reports and employs spatial clustering techniques to identify regions with frequent occurrences. By analyzing both historical trends 
and geographic patterns, the system not only forecasts potential problem areas but also detects recurring issues that indicate underlying structural inefficiencies. Based on these insights, the system generates actionable recommendations aimed at reducing repeated short-term fixes and promoting sustainable solutions.

metadata.title: Smart Citizen Complaint Platform using NLP & Clustering
metadata.description:
The Smart Citizen Complaint Platform is an intelligent system designed to collect, classify, and manage citizens’ complaints efficiently. The platform allows users to submit complaints through a user-friendly interface, where the system automatically analyzes the text using Natural Language Processing (NLP).

The system classifies complaints into categories (such as services, infrastructure, health, etc.) and determines their priority level (normal, urgent, or critical). It may also use clustering techniques to group similar complaints together, helping authorities identify common issues and trends.

Additionally, the platform provides dashboards and visualizations to help decision-makers monitor complaints, track response times, and improve service quality. The main goal is to enhance communication between citizens and authorities while making the complaint-handling process faster, smarter, and more efficient.

This work was conducted at Arab International University (AIU), Syria. The official website of the university is: https://www.aiu.edu.sy

metadata.title: Intelligent Dispatch System: Advancing Geo-Spatial Clustering and Route Optimization
metadata.description:
This paper proposes an Intelligent Dispatch 
System (IDS) to enhance last mile delivery, the most 
complex and costly stage of the supply chain. Leveraging 
machine learning, the system applies DBSCAN clustering 
to group geographically related orders and heuristic 
solvers to address the Traveling Salesman Problem, 
thereby optimizing delivery routes in real time. The 
architecture integrates Node.js telemetry handlers with 
Python-based computation engines and employs MongoDB 
with 2dsphere indexing for efficient geospatial processing. 
A multi-backend framework—covering customers, sellers, 
delivery agents, administrators, and chatbots—ensures 
data consistency and rapid decision-making. Real-world 
scenarios in dense urban networks demonstrate that IDS 
can reduce delivery cycles by 15–30 percent while lowering 
fuel consumption. Beyond operational improvements, the 
system provides mathematical reasoning for AI-driven 
agent allocation and scoring mechanisms, offering a robust 
foundation for retail and e-commerce contexts. By 
combining clustering, geolocation, and optimization 
strategies within a scalable web-based platform, IDS 
illustrates how artificial intelligence can transform last 
mile logistics, improving efficiency, sustainability, and 
customer satisfaction.
keywords: Intelligent Delivery, Dispatch System, Last-Mile DBSCAN, Route Optimization, Artificial Intelligence, Smart Logistics

metadata.title: When QSAR Fails: Dataset Bias, SAR Fragmentation, and the Instability of Analog-Series Benchmarks in HIV-1 Integrase Modeling
metadata.description:
This repository contains the complete computational notebook, curated dataset, and supporting files for the manuscript "When QSAR Fails: Dataset Bias, SAR Fragmentation, and the Instability of Analog-Series Benchmarks in HIV-1 Integrase Modeling."

The notebook (HIV1_Integrase_QSAR_FINAL.ipynb) reproduces all analyses reported in the manuscript, including: data extraction and curation from BindingDB, noise ceiling estimation, molecular descriptor and ECFP4 fingerprint calculation, Bemis-Murcko scaffold and DBSCAN analog series clustering, three-way validation (random, scaffold, analog series splits), DBSCAN sensitivity analysis across six clustering thresholds, series-level learnability analysis, learnability classification, and applicability domain coverage-accuracy tradeoff. All results, figures, and tables in the manuscript can be reproduced by running the notebook in sequence against the BindingDB DuckDB file (available separately from bindingdb.org).

Dependencies: Python 3.10+, RDKit, scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM, DuckDB, pandas, numpy, matplotlib, scipy. See requirements.txt for pinned versions.

creators.name: Salleh, Wan Mohd Nuzul Hakimi Wan

metadata.title: Exploration des données douanières en RDC : Segmentation et détection d'anomalies dans les flux d'importation
metadata.description: Pour passer d'un contrôle systématique à un ciblage intelligent des flux d'importation, cet article explore les données douanières afin de débusquer des profils cachés et des signaux de risque. Nous combinons l'analyse en composantes principales (ACP), la classification hiérarchique ascendante, le K-means et DBSCAN. Les flux douaniers sont complexes : très dimensionnels, déséquilibrés et sensibles aux fraudes variées, comme la sous-évaluation, la fragmentation des colis, des codes SH anormaux, des valeurs unitaires instables, des profils temporels suspects ou des comportements isolés. Les règles strictes actuelles butent sur la « malédiction de la dimensionnalité », entraînant des libérations erronées de déclarations frauduleuses, des saisies injustes et un gaspillage de temps. Nous proposons une approche innovante via une ingénierie de données ciblée. Des indicateurs multidimensionnels d'anomalies sont créés, normalisés, puis simplifiés par l'ACP pour révéler les dimensions clés. Le K-means et la classification hiérarchique segmentent les profils d'importateurs, validés par des tests de qualité et de stabilité. DBSCAN isole les cas atypiques, confirmés par analyse de sensibilité. Les résultats mettent en lumière des profils distincts et un petit nombre d'anomalies inhabituelles, idéales pour un contrôle ciblé. Cette chaîne ACP-clustering-détection d'anomalies fournit un outil clair et opérationnel pour l'analyse de risques douaniers.

This comprehensive anthology consolidates over 500 original working papers and data analysis reports authored by Yasuko Kawahata between 2024 and 2026. The collection covers interdisciplinary research across computational social science, public health, sustainable finance, and urban infrastructure metabolism. This DOI-registered archive serves as a formal record of intellectual property and original analytical methodologies developed using various grant-funded and private datasets. (この包括的なアンソロジーは、2009年から2026年の間に川畑泰子によって執筆された500件以上のオリジナルのワーキングペーパーとデータ分析レポートを統合したものです。このDOI登録されたアーカイブは、知的財産およびオリジナルの分析手法の公式な記録として機能します。)

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

全集導入・解説文およびライセンス情報

【全集の構成と学術的背景に関する解説】 本資料群は、これまでの研究を着想とする2024年から2026年にかけて川畑泰子が構築した、多層的な社会診断および情報生態系に関する研究ノート・解析レポートの集大成である。収録されている500本以上のドキュメントは、以下の5つの主要な学術ドメインが相互に埋め込まれた「知恵の網(Web of Wisdom)」を形成している。

1.     計算社会科学・情報公衆衛生学: フェイクニュースの蔓延メカニズムを「レモンの市場」理論やハイパーグラフ構造から再考した一連の論考は、現代のデジタル空間における「情報的健康」の危機を浮き彫りにしている。特に、誤り訂正符号を用いた言論対立の検出や、ブロックチェーンによる信頼性向上に関する試論は、技術と倫理の交差点における先駆的な取り組みである。

2.     時空間統計による地域経済レジリエンス分析: 全国333業種にわたる高解像度電話帳データを用いたCOVID-19および能登半島地震前後の産業動態分析は、本全集の白眉である。47都道府県を網羅するミクロな登録パルスの解析は、都市のスポンジ化や「薬局砂漠」、さらにはAI曝露下における産業構造の不可逆的な転移をリアルタイムで捉えた、極めて精緻な社会診断記録となっている。

3.     近代日本文学・演劇を通じた精神構造の解読: 夢野久作、久生十蘭、江戸川乱歩、そして唐十郎や寺山修司の作品群に対する言語学的・病理学的分析は、単なる文学批評に留まらない。不在の証明、アイデンティティの消失、身体性の変容といったテーマを通じて、日本人の深層心理と社会構造の歪みを、科学的知見と交差させながら解読している。

4.     サステナブル・ファイナンスと医療福祉インフラ: ESG戦略の効率性評価や、医療・介護資源の空間的アクセシビリティに関する分析は、具体的な社会実装を見据えたエビデンスに基づいている。特に「社会的苦痛指数(SDI)」の提唱や、負の外部性の空間統計学は、次世代の公共政策に向けた強力なパラダイムシフトを提示している。

5.     社会ジオ・メンタルエコシステムの解析: 日本全国の「社会ジオ・メンタルエコシステム」や「生成AIによる産業代謝」「パチンコ・現金化インフラなどの夜の経済圏」といったマクロな社会変容を可視化する、驚異的な計算社会科学のアプローチを提示している。

本全集にDOIを付与し公開することは、これらの膨大な解析知と独創的なアイデアの「初出」を国際的に証明し、不当な盗用や歪曲から知的財産を保護するための、研究者としての決然たるマニフェストである。

【生成AI・LLMsの活用について】 本全集の編纂、膨大な目録の構造化、および一部の解説テキストの校正・フォーマット整形においては、大規模言語モデル(LLMs)等の生成AI技術を補助的ツールとして活用している。ただし、中核となるデータセット、分析モデル、および独創的な学術的考察はすべて著者自身の知見と手作業に基づくものである。

【謝辞(Acknowledgments)】 本全集の完成にあたり、日頃より研究活動を支え、有形無形のサポートをいただいたすべての皆様に心より感謝申し上げる。本研究の基盤となる貴重なデータを提供してくださった各機関、およびオープンサイエンスの理念のもと、本アーカイブの公開を可能にした情報プラットフォームに深く敬意を表する。そして何より、いかなる困難な状況下においても私の知の探求を信じ、日々の生活を温かく支え、共に歩んでくれる家族の存在なしに、この膨大な記録を編纂することは叶わなかった。ここに最大の感謝を捧げる。

Complete Works Introduction, Commentary, and License Information

This collection of materials is a culmination of research notes and analysis reports on multi-layered social diagnosis and information ecosystems, constructed by Yasuko Kawabata from 2024 to 2026. The more than 500 documents included form a "Web of Wisdom" in which the following five major academic domains are interwoven:

 

Computational Social Science and Information Public Health: A series of essays re-examining the mechanisms of fake news spread from the perspective of the "market of lemons" theory and hypergraph structure highlights the crisis of "information health" in the modern digital space. In particular, the essays on detecting discourse conflict using error correction codes and improving reliability through blockchain are pioneering efforts at the intersection of technology and ethics.

 

Spatiotemporal Statistics for Regional Economic Resilience Analysis: The analysis of industrial dynamics before and after COVID-19 and the Noto Peninsula Earthquake, using high-resolution telephone directory data across 333 industries nationwide, is the highlight of this collection. The analysis of micro-level registered pulses covering all 47 prefectures provides an extremely precise social diagnostic record, capturing in real time phenomena such as urban spongification, "pharmacy deserts," and the irreversible transformation of industrial structures under AI exposure.

 

 

Decoding the Mental Structure through Modern Japanese Literature and Theater: The linguistic and pathological analyses of the works of Yumeno Kyusaku, Hisao Juran, Edogawa Ranpo, and the works of Kara Juro and Terayama Shuji go beyond mere literary criticism. Through themes such as proof of absence, loss of identity, and transformation of corporeality, the work deciphers the deep psychology of the Japanese people and distortions in social structure, intersecting them with scientific insights.

 

 

Sustainable Finance and Healthcare and Welfare Infrastructure: The efficiency evaluation of ESG strategies and the analysis of the spatial accessibility of healthcare and long-term care resources are based on evidence with a view to concrete social implementation. In particular, the proposal of the "Social Distress Index (SDI)" and the spatial statistics of negative externalities present a powerful paradigm shift toward next-generation public policy.

 

 

Analysis of Social Geo-Mental Ecosystems: This work presents a remarkable computational social science approach that visualizes macro-level social transformations across Japan, such as "social geo-mental ecosystems," "industrial metabolism driven by generative AI," and "nighttime economic spheres including pachinko and cash-based infrastructure."

 

Assigning a DOI to this complete collection and making it publicly available is a resolute manifesto as a researcher, internationally proving the "first appearance" of this vast analytical knowledge and original ideas, and protecting intellectual property from plagiarism and distortion.

 

[Regarding the Use of Generative AI and LLMs]

Generative AI technologies such as Large-Scale Language Models (LLMs) were used as auxiliary tools in the compilation of this complete collection, the structuring of the extensive catalog, and the proofreading and formatting of some explanatory texts. However, the core datasets, analytical models, and original academic considerations are all based on the author's own knowledge and manual work.

 

[Acknowledgments]

Upon completing this complete collection, I express my heartfelt gratitude to everyone who has supported my research activities and provided tangible and intangible assistance. I deeply respect the institutions that provided the invaluable data forming the foundation of this research, and the information platform that, under the principles of open science, made this archive publicly available. Above all, without the support of my family, who have believed in my pursuit of knowledge even under the most difficult circumstances, warmly supported my daily life, and walked alongside me, compiling this vast record would have been impossible. I offer my deepest gratitude here.
